source_pr.title: *: use a new algorithm to improve the speed of admin check table/index
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #43693

Problem Summary:
In the scenario of very large tables (more than 1 billion rows, more than 1000 regions), after a series of optimizations, adding indexes has become very fast. However, verifying the correctness of the index by admin check table (index) is still slow.

### What is changed and how it works?

1. - Use crc32(md5(concat_ws(0x2, indexColumn))) to check if the data are consistent between the table and index.
    - Group by bucket so that we can locate the inconsistent handle.
    - Continue the compute the checksum if a bucket is found inconsistent between the checksums.
    - If the count of rows in a bucket is lower than a threshold, don't group by anymore, compute the checksum for every row in this bucket.
    - report error if the checksum is inconsistent for a row.
3. Add a variable 'tidb_enable_fast_table_check' to control whether to use the new algorithm and set it's default to true.
4. New algorithm doesn't work for multi-value index and prefixed index.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [x] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
improve the speed of admin check table/index
```
